center.name: Bulletin of the British Ornithologists' Club
center.summary: Bulletin of the British Ornithologists' Club是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联4种鸟类，包括White-tailed tityra、Mountain elaenia、Plain antvireo等4种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The chestnut-sided warbler breeds in Eastern North America, from Saskatchewan to the Maritimes. Although an observation of a male in breeding plumage was recorded as far West as British Columbia. They are usually found south of the 50°N, with the north-west Georgia as the most southern extent of their breeding range. They overwinter in Central America to northern Colombia, with a confirmed sighting from as far south as Ecuador

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: In South America the species primarily inhabits open woodlands, scrublands, and pastures with scattered trees, and some coffee plantations and cloudforest in Colombia. In elevation it in occurs between in Central America, between in Colombia, between in the Venezuelan Andes, and between in the Serranía del Perijá

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Bulletin of the British Ornithologists' Club 127 (2): 118–128. The plain antvireo inhabits a variety of landscapes across its wide range

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Keeney|doi=10.2173/bow.whttit2.01 |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/whttit2/1.0/introduction}} | synonyms = | range_map = Tityra leucura map.svg }} The white-tailed tityra (Tityra leucura) is a medium-sized passerine bird about 19 cm long with pied plumage, mostly white or very pale grey, but with a black crown and wings. It is found in a small area of the Amazon rainforest near the Madeira River in the far west of Brazil close to the Peruvian border
